title: -Contract: Carone and Company, Inc. for the Santa Fe Trackbed to Park Conversion Remediation, Demolition and Perimeter Fencing Project

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution:
1. Approving the plans and specifications for the Santa Fe Trackbed to Park
Conversion Remediation, Demolition and Perimeter Fencing Project; and
2. Accepting the bid of the lowest responsive and responsible bidder, Carone and
Company, Inc.; and
3. Authorizing the City Manager to execute the contract and any amendments,
extensions or other change orders until completion of the project in accordance
with the approved plans and specifications, with Carone and Company, Inc., for
the Santa Fe Trackbed to Park Conversion Remediation, Demolition and
Perimeter Fencing Project from Blake to Ward Street, along Acton Street at 0
Parker Street, 0 Carleton Street, 0 Derby Street, 0 Ward Street, in an amount not
to exceed $3,396,969 which includes a base contract amount of $2,953,886, and
a 15% contingency in the amount of $443,083.
FINANCIAL IMPLICATIONS
Funding for this contract is available in the FY 2026 budget in the One-Time Grant Fund
(Fund 336), the Measure T1 Fund (Fund 511), and the Parks Tax Fund (Fund 138). No
other funding is required, and no other projects will be delayed due to this expenditure.
Construction Contract
Carone and Company, Inc. (lowest bid) $2,953,886
15% Contingency $443,083
Total Construction Cost $3,396,969

sections.current_situation:
In 2021, the City received a $5 million grant from the California Proposition 68
Statewide Parks Program (SPP) to convert four of the undeveloped parcels that
historically served as the Atchison, Topeka and Santa Fe Railroad in South Berkeley
into a new public park.
The Santa Fe Trackbed to Park Conversion Remediation, Demolition and Perimeter
Fencing project is the first phase in transforming the undeveloped parcels into a new
public park. The project will remediate contaminated soils and install new perimeter
fencing along the property lines, thereby preparing the site for construction of the new
public park.
The project is funded by grant funds from the California Proposition 68 Statewide Parks
Program (SPP) and Measure T1, the 2016 Berkeley voter-approved bond program to
pay for improvements to City infrastructure and facilities, and Parks Tax.
sections.background:
The project was advertised for bids on Thursday, November 20, 2025, and bids were
opened on Thursday, December 18, 2025. The City received 11 bids, from a low base
bid of $2,953,886 to a high base bid of $4,699,667. With the exception of the lowest bid,
all bids were above the amount budgeted for this project. The determination of the
lowest responsive and responsible bidder was based on the price for base bid work, as
indicated in the bid documents. Carone and Company, Inc. was the lowest responsive
and responsible bidder. Staff conducted reference checks and received satisfactory
feedback. Staff recommends that a contract for this project be awarded to Carone and
Company, Inc.
The Living Wage Ordinance does not apply to this project since construction contracts
are, pursuant to City policy, subject to State prevailing wage laws. The contractor will
need to submit a certification of Compliance with Equal Benefits Ordinance. The
contract will be subject to the Community Workforce Agreement.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
The project will remediate contaminated soils at the site and the contract includes
requirements to comply with the City’s Environmentally Preferable Purchasing Policy.
sections.rationale:
The Santa Fe Trackbed to Park Conversion Remediation, Demolition, and Perimeter
Fencing Project is needed to advance construction of the new four-block park funded by

a California Proposition 68 Statewide Parks Program (SPP) grant awarded to the City.
The City does not have in-house labor or equipment resources to complete this
remediation project.

Page 3

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: CARONE AND COMPANY, INC. FOR SANTA FE TRACKBED TO PARK
CONVERSION REMEDIATION, DEMOLITION AND PERIMETER FENCING PROJECT
WHEREAS, the Santa Fe Trackbed to Park Conversion Remediation, Demolition and
Perimeter Fencing Project is a One-Time Grant and Measure T1 bond project; and
WHEREAS, the City has neither the in-house labor nor the equipment necessary to
undertake this project; and
WHEREAS, invitation for bids was duly advertised on November 20, 2025, bids were
opened on December 18, 2025, and the City received 11 bids; and
WHEREAS, Carone and Company, Inc. was determined to be the lowest responsive
and responsible bidder, and references for Carone and Company, Inc. were provided
and were satisfactory; and
WHEREAS, funding is available in the FY 2026 budget in the One-Time Grant Fund
(Fund 336), the Measure T1 Fund (Fund 511), and the Parks Tax Fund (Fund 138).
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
Council approves the Plans and Specifications for Specification No. 26-11756-C for the
Santa Fe Trackbed to Park Conversion Remediation, Demolition and Perimeter Fencing
Project; and
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the construction contract is awarded to Carone and
Company, Inc. as the lowest responsive and responsible bidder; and
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the City Manager is authorized to execute the
contract and any amendments, extensions, or change orders until completion of the
project in accordance with the approved plans and specifications with Carone and
Company, Inc. for the Santa Fe Trackbed to Park Conversion Remediation, Demolition
and Perimeter Fencing Project in an amount not to exceed $3,396,969, which includes
a contract amount of $2,953,886 and a 15% contingency in the amount of $443,083 for
unforeseen circumstances. A record signature copy of the agreement and any
amendments to be on file in the Office of the City Clerk.
Page 5 of 5
A
